content: Full-viewport overlay isolation pattern: a single fixed-position div (position:fixed; inset:0 or top/left/right/bottom:0) appended to document.body at init, given pointer-events:none by default so host page interaction passes through untouched; only the specific rendered response element inside gets pointer-events:auto to become clickable while the rest of the overlay stays inert. z-index should be a single high-but-not-absurd value (industry guidance: avoid 999999-style values; pick one coordinated max value, e.g. 2147483647 max int is the common 'always on top' ceiling for third-party widgets since host apps cannot exceed it, though moderate values like 999999 are typically sufficient) to sit above the host's own stacking contexts. Shadow DOM is recommended over an iframe for this use case when the overlay needs to inherit host fonts/visually blend, since Shadow DOM still inherits font-family and other inherited CSS from the document root while blocking host CSS rules from leaking in; iframe gives stronger isolation but loses font inheritance and requires postMessage for all communication and sizing coordination.
